evidence_excerpt: After the operation, weakness in the lower extremities gradually improved; at 38 months motor power was MRC 3/5, but she could not walk and had poor control of urinary and fecal functions. Follow-up MRI at 19 months showed an unchanged residual lesion with decreased enhancement.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent neurological deficits with inability to walk and poor bladder and bowel control at long-term follow-up.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After the operation, weakness in the lower extremities gradually improved.
At 38 months after surgery, motor power in the lower extremities improved to MRC Grade 3/5.
At 38 months after surgery, she could not walk and had poor control of urinary and fecal functions.

notes_on_uncertainty: Definitive diagnosis was confirmed postoperatively via pathology; follow-up intervals are reported relative to surgery. Index time approximated to the hospital admission/surgical episode. Early postoperative status was unchanged; timing of the initial improvement is not precisely specified.
